Cannot contribute to Emerging_Trends coverage from this fetch."}, {"url": "https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/nutrition/articles/10.3389/fnut.2024.1342787/full", "title": "The impact of intermittent fasting on gut microbiota: a systematic review of human studies", "summary": "This 2024 Frontiers in Nutrition systematic review (PROSPERO CRD42021241619, PRISMA2020) synthesizes 8 human studies examining how intermittent fasting (IF) subtypes — time-restricted eating (TRE, 12-18h fasting windows), alternate-day fasting (ADF, ~25% energy on fast days), and the 5:2 diet — modulate gut microbiota richness, alpha/beta diversity, and composition. Of 1,332 records screened across Cochrane, PubMed, Scopus and Web of Science (940 after dedup), 8 were included: 6 TRE, 1 ADF, 1 5:2. Most studies reported IF-associated increases in richness and alpha diversity, with compositional shifts, though specific responder taxa varied widely between studies (heterogeneous). The review highlights that IF's metabolic benefits in obesity and metabolic syndrome may be partially mediated by the microbiota via SCFA, glucose-homeostasis and inflammation pathways. Notably, one included RCT (Guo et al. 2021, China, n=39 completers, 5:2 vs control, 30-50y) enrolled metabolic-syndrome patients specifically and reported improved cardiometabolic risk factors alongside microbiota alterations. Authors call for more standardized trials to determine whether compositional changes are durably beneficial.", "perspectives": ["Emerging_Trends", "Scientific", "Methodological"], "key_facts": ["Systematic review included 8 human studies (6 TRE, 1 ADF, 1 5:2) from 1,332 screened records, 940 post-dedup; PROSPERO CRD42021241619", "Guo et al. 2021 RCT in China enrolled metabolic-syndrome patients aged 30-50y (n=39 completers: 21 5:2 diet, 18 control) using a 75%-energy-restriction 5:2 protocol and reported improved cardiometabolic risk factors plus altered gut microbiota", "IF protocols defined: TRE 12-18h daily fasts (zero-cal drinks allowed); ADF ~25% energy on fast days; 5:2 modified ADF (2 non-consecutive fasting days/week)", "Most included studies reported statistically significant increases in alpha diversity and richness, but responder taxa differed widely between trials (substantial heterogeneity)", "Proposed mechanisms: reduced free-radical production, improved glucose homeostasis, augmented stress resistance and suppressed inflammation, partly mediated by microbial SCFA metabolites"], "knowledge_contribution": "Establishes Emerging_Trends coverage for the gut-microbiome mechanism linking IF to metabolic-syndrome benefits. Provides the first human-only systematic review quantifying alpha-diversity/richness shifts across TRE/ADF/5:2 protocols and explicitly names a metabolic-syndrome RCT (Guo 2021) tying 5:2 fasting to cardiometabolic improvements via microbiota remodeling."}, {"url": "https://www.researchgate.net/publication/380166610_Intermittent_fasting_modulates_human_gut_microbiota_diversity_in_a_phenotype-dependent_manner_a_systematic_review", "title": "Intermittent fasting modulates human gut microbiota diversity in a phenotype-dependent manner: a systematic review", "summary": "INSUFFICIENT_CONTENT: paywalled/shell", "perspectives": [], "key_facts": [], "knowledge_contribution": "ResearchGate Cloudflare security check returned; no article content accessible.
